description:
fs2 is used in exactly one non-test file, src/store_lock.rs, for FileExt::lock_exclusive() and FileExt::unlock(). std::fs::File::lock(), try_lock(), and unlock() were stabilized in Rust 1.89; verified to compile on the project toolchain (rustc 1.94). There is no rust-version/MSRV pin in Cargo.toml, so nothing blocks the swap.

WHY: zero-cost win. Removes the fs2 crate from the tree for a ~5-line shim change in store_lock.rs. lock_exclusive() -> lock(); unlock() stays unlock(). libc is unaffected (still pulled by the plugin code), so no transitive regression.

SCOPE: replace the fs2::FileExt import and the two call sites in src/store_lock.rs with the std equivalents; drop fs2 from Cargo.toml [dependencies]; confirm Cargo.lock no longer lists fs2; full test suite + 100% coverage gate must pass. If adding an MSRV pin is warranted as a side effect, note it but it is not required by this task.
title: Drop fs2: use std::fs::File::lock/try_lock/unlock
